name: Sullivan v. Florida
question: Does the imposition of a life sentence without parole on a 13 year old convicted of a non-homicidal offense violate the Eighth and Fourteenth Amendments' prohibition of "cruel and unusual punishment?"
description: A case, which the Court dismissed as improvidently granted, concerning the constitutionality of imposing a life sentence with no chance of parole on a 13-year-old convicted of a non-homicidal offense.
